questions.question: What is a tangent to a circle?
questions.correct_answer: A tangent to a circle is a line that intersects the circle at exactly one point
questions.explanation: A tangent touches the circle at only one point, unlike a secant which intersects it at two points.

questions.question: What is the point of contact of a tangent?
questions.correct_answer: The common point where the tangent touches the circle
questions.explanation: It is the single point shared by the tangent and the circle.

questions.question: State Theorem 10.1 related to tangents.
questions.correct_answer: The tangent at any point of a circle is perpendicular to the radius through the point of contact
questions.explanation: This theorem establishes the right angle between the radius and the tangent.

questions.question: How many tangents can be drawn at a point on a circle?
questions.explanation: At any point on the circle, exactly one tangent is possible.

questions.question: How many tangents can be drawn from a point inside a circle?
questions.explanation: All lines through an interior point intersect the circle at two points.

questions.question: How many tangents can be drawn from a point outside a circle?
questions.explanation: From an external point, exactly two tangents can be drawn to a circle.

questions.question: State Theorem 10.2 related to tangents from an external point.
questions.correct_answer: The lengths of tangents drawn from an external point to a circle are equal
questions.explanation: Both tangents from the same external point have equal lengths.

questions.question: What is meant by the length of a tangent?
questions.correct_answer: The distance between the external point and the point of contact
questions.explanation: It is the segment of the tangent from the external point to the circle.

questions.question: What is a secant of a circle?
questions.correct_answer: A line that intersects the circle at two points
questions.explanation: A secant cuts the circle, unlike a tangent which only touches it.

questions.question: What is the relation between a tangent and a secant?
questions.correct_answer: A tangent is a special case of a secant when the two points of intersection coincide
questions.explanation: When both intersection points merge into one, the secant becomes a tangent.

notes.title: Tangent to a Circle
notes.content: A tangent touches a circle at exactly one point and never enters its interior.

notes.title: Perpendicular Property
notes.content: The radius through the point of contact is always perpendicular to the tangent.

notes.title: Equal Tangents
notes.content: Tangents drawn from the same external point are always equal in length.

notes.title: Number of Tangents
notes.content: 0 tangents from inside, 1 from on the circle, and 2 from outside the circle.

notes.title: Point of Contact
notes.content: The single point where the tangent touches the circle.

facts.fact: A circle can have infinitely many tangents.
No tangent can pass through a point inside the circle.
The radius is the shortest distance from the centre to the tangent.
Tangents from an external point form an isosceles triangle with the radii.
